authors_text: Minbyul Jeong,Jaewoo Kang
title: Enhancing Label Consistency on Document-level Named Entity Recognition
title_zh: 增强文档级命名实体识别的标签一致性
abstract: Named entity recognition (NER) is a fundamental part of extracting information from documents in biomedical applications. A notable advantage of NER is its consistency in extracting biomedical entities in a document context. Although existing document NER models show consistent predictions, they still do not meet our expectations. We investigated whether the adjectives and prepositions within an entity cause a low label consistency, which results in inconsistent predictions. In this paper, we present our method, ConNER, which enhances the label dependency of modifiers (e.g., adjectives and prepositions) to achieve higher label agreement. ConNER refines the draft labels of the modifiers to improve the output representations of biomedical entities. The effectiveness of our method is demonstrated on four popular biomedical NER datasets; in particular, its efficacy is proved on two datasets with 7.5-8.6% absolute improvements in the F1 score. We interpret that our ConNER method is effective on datasets that have intrinsically low label consistency. In the qualitative analysis, we demonstrate how our approach makes the NER model generate consistent predictions. Our code and resources are available at https://github.com/dmis-lab/ConNER/.
summary: Named entity recognition (NER) is a fundamental part of extracting information from documents in biomedical applications. A notable advantage of NER is its consistency in extracting biomedical entities in a document context. Although existing document NER models show consistent predictions, they still do not meet our expectations. We investigated whether the adjectives and prepositions within an entity cause a low label consistency, which results in inconsistent predictions. In this paper, we present our method, ConNER, which enhances the label dependency of modifiers (e.g., adjectives and prepositions) to achieve higher label agreement. ConNER refines the draft labels of the modifiers to improve the output representations of biomedical entities. The effectiveness of our method is demonstrated on four popular biomedical NER datasets; in particular, its efficacy is proved on two datasets with 7.5-8.6% absolute improvements in the F1 score. We interpret that our ConNER method is effective on datasets that have intrinsically low label consistency. In the qualitative analysis, we demonstrate how our approach makes the NER model generate consistent predictions. Our code and resources are available at https://github.com/dmis-lab/ConNER/.
abstract_zh: 命名实体识别 (NER) 是从生物医学应用中的文档中提取信息的基本部分。 NER 的一个显着优势是它在文档上下文中提取生物医学实体的一致性。尽管现有的文档 NER 模型显示出一致的预测，但它们仍然没有达到我们的预期。我们调查了实体中的形容词和介词是否会导致标签一致性较低，从而导致预测不一致。在本文中，我们提出了我们的方法 ConNER，它增强了修饰语（例如形容词和介词）的标签依赖性，以实现更高的标签一致性。 ConNER 细化了修饰符的草稿标签，以改进生物医学实体的输出表示。我们的方法的有效性在四个流行的生物医学 NER 数据集上得到了证明；特别是，它的有效性在两个数据集上得到了证明，F1 分数绝对提高了 7.5-8.6%。我们解释说，我们的 ConNER 方法对于本质上标签一致性较低的数据集是有效的。在定性分析中，我们展示了我们的方法如何使 NER 模型生成一致的预测。我们的代码和资源可在 https://github.com/dmis-lab/ConNER/ 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过改进修饰词（如形容词和介词）的标签依赖性，提升生物医学文档级命名实体识别中的标签一致性。
AI.motivation: 现有文档级命名实体识别模型在预测一致性上仍存在不足，尤其修饰词可能导致标签不一致，需针对性优化。
AI.method: 提出ConNER方法，通过细化修饰词的初稿标签，增强其与实体表示的关联性，从而提升整体标签一致性。
AI.result: 在四个生物医学NER数据集上验证，其中两个数据集的F1分数提升7.5-8.6%，尤其在标签一致性较低的数据集上效果显著。
AI.conclusion: ConNER通过强化修饰词标签依赖性，有效解决了低一致性数据集的标签冲突问题，定性分析验证了其一致性提升机制。

We interpret that our ConNER method is effective on datasets that have intrinsically low label consistency. In the qualitative analysis, we demonstrate how our approach makes the NER model generate consistent predictions. Our code and resources are available at https://github.com/dmis-lab/ConNER/. 命名实体识别 (NER) 是从生物医学应用中的文档中提取信息的基本部分。 NER 的一个显着优势是它在文档上下文中提取生物医学实体的一致性。尽管现有的文档 NER 模型显示出一致的预测，但它们仍然没有达到我们的预期。我们调查了实体中的形容词和介词是否会导致标签一致性较低，从而导致预测不一致。在本文中，我们提出了我们的方法 ConNER，它增强了修饰语（例如形容词和介词）的标签依赖性，以实现更高的标签一致性。 ConNER 细化了修饰符的草稿标签，以改进生物医学实体的输出表示。我们的方法的有效性在四个流行的生物医学 NER 数据集上得到了证明；特别是，它的有效性在两个数据集上得到了证明，F1 分数绝对提高了 7.5-8.6%。我们解释说，我们的 ConNER 方法对于本质上标签一致性较低的数据集是有效的。在定性分析中，我们展示了我们的方法如何使 NER 模型生成一致的预测。我们的代码和资源可在 https://github.com/dmis-lab/ConNER/ 获取。 本文旨在通过改进修饰词（如形容词和介词）的标签依赖性，提升生物医学文档级命名实体识别中的标签一致性。 Minbyul Jeong,Jaewoo Kang cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG

authors_text: Shima Rashidi,Ruwan Tennakoon,Aref Miri Rekavandi,Papangkorn Jessadatavornwong,Amanda Freis,Garret Huff,Mark Easton,Adrian Mouritz,Reza Hoseinnezhad,Alireza Bab-Hadiashar
title: IT-RUDA: Information Theory Assisted Robust Unsupervised Domain Adaptation
title_zh: IT-RUDA：信息论辅助鲁棒无监督域适应
abstract: Distribution shift between train (source) and test (target) datasets is a common problem encountered in machine learning applications. One approach to resolve this issue is to use the Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) technique that carries out knowledge transfer from a label-rich source domain to an unlabeled target domain. Outliers that exist in either source or target datasets can introduce additional challenges when using UDA in practice. In this paper, $α$-divergence is used as a measure to minimize the discrepancy between the source and target distributions while inheriting robustness, adjustable with a single parameter $α$, as the prominent feature of this measure. Here, it is shown that the other well-known divergence-based UDA techniques can be derived as special cases of the proposed method. Furthermore, a theoretical upper bound is derived for the loss in the target domain in terms of the source loss and the initial $α$-divergence between the two domains. The robustness of the proposed method is validated through testing on several benchmarked datasets in open-set and partial UDA setups where extra classes existing in target and source datasets are considered as outliers.
summary: Distribution shift between train (source) and test (target) datasets is a common problem encountered in machine learning applications. One approach to resolve this issue is to use the Unsupervised Domain Adaptation (UDA) technique that carries out knowledge transfer from a label-rich source domain to an unlabeled target domain. Outliers that exist in either source or target datasets can introduce additional challenges when using UDA in practice. In this paper, $α$-divergence is used as a measure to minimize the discrepancy between the source and target distributions while inheriting robustness, adjustable with a single parameter $α$, as the prominent feature of this measure. Here, it is shown that the other well-known divergence-based UDA techniques can be derived as special cases of the proposed method. Furthermore, a theoretical upper bound is derived for the loss in the target domain in terms of the source loss and the initial $α$-divergence between the two domains. The robustness of the proposed method is validated through testing on several benchmarked datasets in open-set and partial UDA setups where extra classes existing in target and source datasets are considered as outliers.
abstract_zh: 训练（源）和测试（目标）数据集之间的分布变化是机器学习应用中遇到的常见问题。解决此问题的一种方法是使用无监督域适应 (UDA) 技术，该技术将知识从富含标签的源域转移到未标记的目标域。在实践中使用 UDA 时，源数据集或目标数据集中存在的异常值可能会带来额外的挑战。在本文中，$α$-散度被用作最小化源分布和目标分布之间的差异的度量，同时继承鲁棒性，可通过单个参数$α$进行调整，作为该度量的显着特征。这里表明，其他众所周知的基于散度的 UDA 技术可以作为所提出方法的特殊情况导出。此外，根据源损失和两个域之间的初始 $α$ 散度，推导了目标域中损失的理论上限。通过在开放集和部分 UDA 设置中对多个基准数据集进行测试来验证所提出方法的稳健性，其中目标和源数据集中存在的额外类被视为异常值。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于信息论的鲁棒无监督领域自适应方法IT-RUDA，通过α-散度度量最小化源域与目标域分布差异，并处理异常值问题。
AI.motivation: 领域自适应中源域与目标域分布偏移导致模型性能下降，而现有方法对异常值敏感且缺乏灵活性。
AI.method: 采用α-散度作为分布差异度量，通过调整参数α实现鲁棒性；推导目标域损失的理论上限，并证明其他UDA方法为本方法特例。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集的开放集和部分UDA实验中，验证了方法对异常值的鲁棒性及优于现有方法的适应效果。
AI.conclusion: IT-RUDA通过理论分析和实验验证，为处理领域自适应中的分布偏移和异常值问题提供了有效且灵活的解决方案。

authors_text: Xili Dai,Mingyang Li,Pengyuan Zhai,Shengbang Tong,Xingjian Gao,Shao-Lun Huang,Zhihui Zhu,Chong You,Yi Ma
title: Revisiting Sparse Convolutional Model for Visual Recognition
title_zh: 重新审视视觉识别的稀疏卷积模型
abstract: Despite strong empirical performance for image classification, deep neural networks are often regarded as ``black boxes'' and they are difficult to interpret. On the other hand, sparse convolutional models, which assume that a signal can be expressed by a linear combination of a few elements from a convolutional dictionary, are powerful tools for analyzing natural images with good theoretical interpretability and biological plausibility. However, such principled models have not demonstrated competitive performance when compared with empirically designed deep networks. This paper revisits the sparse convolutional modeling for image classification and bridges the gap between good empirical performance (of deep learning) and good interpretability (of sparse convolutional models). Our method uses differentiable optimization layers that are defined from convolutional sparse coding as drop-in replacements of standard convolutional layers in conventional deep neural networks. We show that such models have equally strong empirical performance on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet datasets when compared to conventional neural networks. By leveraging stable recovery property of sparse modeling, we further show that such models can be much more robust to input corruptions as well as adversarial perturbations in testing through a simple proper trade-off between sparse regularization and data reconstruction terms. Source code can be found at https://github.com/Delay-Xili/SDNet.
summary: Despite strong empirical performance for image classification, deep neural networks are often regarded as ``black boxes'' and they are difficult to interpret. On the other hand, sparse convolutional models, which assume that a signal can be expressed by a linear combination of a few elements from a convolutional dictionary, are powerful tools for analyzing natural images with good theoretical interpretability and biological plausibility. However, such principled models have not demonstrated competitive performance when compared with empirically designed deep networks. This paper revisits the sparse convolutional modeling for image classification and bridges the gap between good empirical performance (of deep learning) and good interpretability (of sparse convolutional models). Our method uses differentiable optimization layers that are defined from convolutional sparse coding as drop-in replacements of standard convolutional layers in conventional deep neural networks. We show that such models have equally strong empirical performance on CIFAR-10, CIFAR-100, and ImageNet datasets when compared to conventional neural networks. By leveraging stable recovery property of sparse modeling, we further show that such models can be much more robust to input corruptions as well as adversarial perturbations in testing through a simple proper trade-off between sparse regularization and data reconstruction terms. Source code can be found at https://github.com/Delay-Xili/SDNet.
abstract_zh: 尽管图像分类具有很强的经验性能，但深度神经网络通常被视为“黑匣子”并且难以解释。另一方面，稀疏卷积模型假设信号可以通过卷积字典中几个元素的线性组合来表示，是分析自然图像的强大工具，具有良好的理论可解释性和生物学合理性。然而，与凭经验设计的深度网络相比，这种原理性模型并未表现出有竞争力的性能。本文重新审视了图像分类的稀疏卷积模型，并弥合了良好的经验性能（深度学习）和良好的可解释性（稀疏卷积模型）之间的差距。我们的方法使用从卷积稀疏编码定义的可微优化层作为传统深度神经网络中标准卷积层的直接替代品。我们表明，与传统神经网络相比，此类模型在 CIFAR-10、CIFAR-100 和 ImageNet 数据集上具有同样强大的实证性能。通过利用稀疏建模的稳定恢复特性，我们进一步表明，通过稀疏正则化和数据重建项之间的简单适当权衡，此类模型对于输入损坏以及测试中的对抗性扰动具有更强的鲁棒性。源代码可以在 https://github.com/Delay-Xili/SDNet 找到。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过改进稀疏卷积模型，使其在图像分类任务中达到与深度神经网络相当的性能，同时保持良好的可解释性。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络虽性能强大但缺乏可解释性，而稀疏卷积模型虽具理论可解释性但性能不足，需弥合两者间的差距。
AI.method: 采用可微分优化层替代传统卷积层，基于卷积稀疏编码构建模型，并通过稀疏正则化与数据重建项的平衡提升鲁棒性。
AI.result: 在CIFAR-10/100和ImageNet数据集上性能与传统网络相当，且对输入噪声和对抗扰动具有更强的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 稀疏卷积模型可通过优化设计兼顾高性能与可解释性，为视觉识别提供更可靠且透明的解决方案。

authors_text: Prabath Abeysekara,Hai Dong,A. K. Qin
title: Deep Edge Intelligence: Architecture, Key Features, Enabling Technologies and Challenges
title_zh: 深边缘智能：架构、主要特性、支持技术和挑战
abstract: With the breakthroughs in Deep Learning, recent years have witnessed a massive surge in Artificial Intelligence applications and services. Meanwhile, the rapid advances in Mobile Computing and Internet of Things has also given rise to billions of mobile and smart sensing devices connected to the Internet, generating zettabytes of data at the network edge. The opportunity to combine these two domains of technologies to power interconnected devices with intelligence is likely to pave the way for a new wave of technology revolutions. Embracing this technology revolution, in this article, we present a novel computing vision named Deep Edge Intelligence (DEI). DEI employs Deep Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud and Edge Computing, 5G/6G networks, Internet of Things, Microservices, etc. aiming to provision reliable and secure intelligence services to every person and organisation at any place with better user experience. The vision, system architecture, key layers and features of DEI are also detailed. Finally, we reveal the key enabling technologies and research challenges associated with it.
summary: With the breakthroughs in Deep Learning, recent years have witnessed a massive surge in Artificial Intelligence applications and services. Meanwhile, the rapid advances in Mobile Computing and Internet of Things has also given rise to billions of mobile and smart sensing devices connected to the Internet, generating zettabytes of data at the network edge. The opportunity to combine these two domains of technologies to power interconnected devices with intelligence is likely to pave the way for a new wave of technology revolutions. Embracing this technology revolution, in this article, we present a novel computing vision named Deep Edge Intelligence (DEI). DEI employs Deep Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud and Edge Computing, 5G/6G networks, Internet of Things, Microservices, etc. aiming to provision reliable and secure intelligence services to every person and organisation at any place with better user experience. The vision, system architecture, key layers and features of DEI are also detailed. Finally, we reveal the key enabling technologies and research challenges associated with it.
abstract_zh: 随着深度学习的突破，近年来人工智能应用和服务大幅增长。与此同时，移动计算和物联网的快速发展也催生了数十亿个连接到互联网的移动和智能传感设备，在网络边缘产生了泽字节的数据。将这两个技术领域结合起来为互联设备提供智能的机会可能会为新一波的技术革命铺平道路。拥抱这场技术革命，在本文中，我们提出了一种名为 Deep Edge Intelligence (DEI) 的新颖计算愿景。 DEI采用深度学习、人工智能、云与边缘计算、5G/6G网络、物联网、微服务等技术，旨在为任何地点的每个人、每个组织提供可靠、安全的智能服务，并提供更好的用户体验。还详细介绍了DEI的愿景、系统架构、关键层和功能。最后，我们揭示了关键的支持技术和与之相关的研究挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为深度边缘智能（DEI）的新计算愿景，旨在通过结合深度学习、云计算、边缘计算等技术，为用户提供可靠且安全的智能化服务。
AI.motivation: 随着人工智能和物联网设备的爆炸性增长，网络边缘产生了海量数据，亟需融合深度学习与边缘计算技术以提升智能服务的效率和用户体验。
AI.method: 通过分析深度学习、云计算、5G/6G网络、微服务等核心技术，构建DEI的系统架构，详细阐述其关键层级、特征及使能技术。
AI.result: 明确了DEI的愿景与系统架构，总结了其核心特征和关键技术，并指出了在安全性、延迟优化及资源管理等方面的挑战。
AI.conclusion: DEI为未来智能服务提供了新方向，但需进一步解决技术融合、边缘设备算力限制及跨领域协同等核心难题。

search_text: Deep Edge Intelligence: Architecture, Key Features, Enabling Technologies and Challenges 深边缘智能：架构、主要特性、支持技术和挑战 With the breakthroughs in Deep Learning, recent years have witnessed a massive surge in Artificial Intelligence applications and services. Meanwhile, the rapid advances in Mobile Computing and Internet of Things has also given rise to billions of mobile and smart sensing devices connected to the Internet, generating zettabytes of data at the network edge. The opportunity to combine these two domains of technologies to power interconnected devices with intelligence is likely to pave the way for a new wave of technology revolutions. Embracing this technology revolution, in this article, we present a novel computing vision named Deep Edge Intelligence (DEI). DEI employs Deep Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Cloud and Edge Computing, 5G/6G networks, Internet of Things, Microservices, etc. aiming to provision reliable and secure intelligence services to every person and organisation at any place with better user experience. The vision, system architecture, key layers and features of DEI are also detailed.
